component_identification.content: Main forest board with sun rotation track, cardboard trees in three sizes plus seeds for each player color, sunlight point tokens, scoring tiles for different richness levels, and a sun marker. Trees occupy hexes of varying soil richness and cast shadows in changing directions.

core_game_loop.content: Players spend sunlight points to seed, grow, and eventually cut down trees for score. After all players act, the sun rotates, producing new sunlight based on unshadowed trees. Because shadow direction changes each round, timing and positioning are the core puzzle.

detailed_rules.content: Each turn, a player may perform any number of actions while paying sunlight costs: buy from supply, seed from existing trees, grow seed to small to medium to large, and cut down large trees for scoring. Seeding distance equals source tree size, and seeds cannot pass through occupied spaces. When the sun activates, each unshadowed tree grants sunlight equal to its size; trees in shadow of equal or larger height produce nothing. After every full round, rotate the sun marker one step, changing shadow lines for future turns. Scoring tiles for each richness ring decline in value over time, rewarding earlier harvests of prime spots but potentially stronger long-term engines if delayed. Supply management matters: pieces moved off board go to cycle tracks and can be repurchased.

scoring_and_endgame.content: The game ends after the sun completes its final rotation cycle. Players total points from collected scoring tiles plus leftover sunlight converted at a low rate. Highest total wins; ties are broken by remaining trees/seeds per rulebook tiebreaker.

beginner_strategy.content: Occupy rich center-adjacent soil early, but do not overcluster where one opponent can shadow multiple trees. Build a steady income curve: at least a few medium/large trees by midgame. Harvest at least some rich tiles early before values drop too far. Track next round’s sun direction before committing growth. Use seeding to threaten future lanes and block key hexes, not just to expand blindly.
